<content>1: package taskstate
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 	"os"
6: 	"path/filepath"
7: 	"sort"
8: 	"strings"
9: 	"time"
10: 
11: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config/taskstore"
12: )
13: 
14: type Status string
15: 
16: const (
17: 	StatusReady     Status = "ready"
18: 	StatusDone      Status = "done"
19: 	StatusReviewing Status = "reviewing"
20: 	StatusCancelled Status = "cancelled"
21: 
22: 	// Lifecycle-stage statuses — canonical names used by the FSM.
23: 	StatusPlanning     Status = "planning"
24: 	StatusImplementing Status = "implementing"
25: )
26: 
27: type TaskEntry struct {
28: 	Status        Status    `json:"status"`
29: 	Description   string    `json:"description,omitempty"`
30: 	Branch        string    `json:"branch,omitempty"`
31: 	Topic         string    `json:"topic,omitempty"`
32: 	CreatedAt     time.Time `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
33: 	Implemented   string    `json:"implemented,omitempty"`
34: 	ClickUpTaskID string    `json:"clickup_task_id,omitempty"`
35: 	ReviewCycle   int       `json:"review_cycle,omitempty"`
36: }
37: 
38: type TopicEntry struct {
39: 	CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
40: }
41: 
42: type TaskState struct {
43: 	Dir          string
44: 	Plans        map[string]TaskEntry
45: 	TopicEntries map[string]TopicEntry
46: 	store        taskstore.Store // always non-nil
47: 	project      string          // project name used with the store
48: }
49: 
50: type TaskInfo struct {
51: 	Filename    string
52: 	Status      Status
53: 	Description string
54: 	Branch      string
55: 	Topic       string
56: 	CreatedAt   time.Time
57: }
58: 
59: type TopicInfo struct {
60: 	Name      string
61: 	CreatedAt time.Time
62: }
63: 
64: // Load creates a TaskState backed by the given store. Plans and TopicEntries are
65: // populated from the store. dir is retained for file operations (e.g. Rename moves
66: // the .md file on disk). The store is always required — there is no JSON fallback.
67: func Load(store taskstore.Store, project, dir string) (*TaskState, error) {
68: 	plans, err := store.List(project)
69: 	if err != nil {
70: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("task store: %w", err)
71: 	}
72: 
73: 	topics, err := store.ListTopics(project)
74: 	if err != nil {
75: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("task store: %w", err)
76: 	}
77: 
78: 	ps := &TaskState{
79: 		Dir:          dir,
80: 		Plans:        make(map[string]TaskEntry, len(plans)),
81: 		TopicEntries: make(map[string]TopicEntry, len(topics)),
82: 		store:        store,
83: 		project:      project,
84: 	}
85: 
86: 	for _, e := range plans {
87: 		ps.Plans[e.Filename] = TaskEntry{
88: 			Status:        Status(e.Status),
89: 			Description:   e.Description,
90: 			Branch:        e.Branch,
91: 			Topic:         e.Topic,
92: 			CreatedAt:     e.CreatedAt,
93: 			Implemented:   e.Implemented,
94: 			ClickUpTaskID: e.ClickUpTaskID,
95: 			ReviewCycle:   e.ReviewCycle,
96: 		}
97: 	}
98: 
99: 	for _, t := range topics {
100: 		ps.TopicEntries[t.Name] = TopicEntry{CreatedAt: t.CreatedAt}
101: 	}
102: 
103: 	return ps, nil
104: }
105: 
106: // Topics returns all topic entries sorted by name.
107: func (ps *TaskState) Topics() []TopicInfo {
108: 	// Discover topics from both TopicEntries and plan topic fields.
109: 	seen := make(map[string]TopicInfo)
110: 	for name, entry := range ps.TopicEntries {
111: 		seen[name] = TopicInfo{Name: name, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt}
112: 	}
113: 	for _, entry := range ps.Plans {
114: 		if entry.Topic != "" {
115: 			if _, ok := seen[entry.Topic]; !ok {
116: 				seen[entry.Topic] = TopicInfo{Name: entry.Topic}
117: 			}
118: 		}
119: 	}
120: 	result := make([]TopicInfo, 0, len(seen))
121: 	for _, info := range seen {
122: 		result = append(result, info)
123: 	}
124: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
125: 		return result[i].Name < result[j].Name
126: 	})
127: 	return result
128: }
129: 
130: // TasksByTopic returns all plans in the given topic, sorted by filename.
131: func (ps *TaskState) TasksByTopic(topic string) []TaskInfo {
132: 	result := make([]TaskInfo, 0)
133: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
134: 		if entry.Topic == topic {
135: 			result = append(result, TaskInfo{
136: 				Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
137: 				Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
138: 				Topic: entry.Topic, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
139: 			})
140: 		}
141: 	}
142: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
143: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
144: 	})
145: 	return result
146: }
147: 
148: // UngroupedTasks returns all active plans with no topic, sorted by filename.
149: func (ps *TaskState) UngroupedTasks() []TaskInfo {
150: 	result := make([]TaskInfo, 0)
151: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
152: 		if entry.Status == StatusDone || entry.Status == StatusCancelled {
153: 			continue
154: 		}
155: 		if entry.Topic == "" {
156: 			result = append(result, TaskInfo{
157: 				Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
158: 				Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
159: 				CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
160: 			})
161: 		}
162: 	}
163: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
164: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
165: 	})
166: 	return result
167: }
168: 
169: // hasTopicEntry returns true if the given topic name exists in TopicEntries.
170: func (ps *TaskState) hasTopicEntry(topic string) bool {
171: 	if ps.TopicEntries == nil {
172: 		return false
173: 	}
174: 	_, exists := ps.TopicEntries[topic]
175: 	return exists
176: }
177: 
178: // HasRunningCoderInTopic checks if any plan in the given topic (other than
179: // excludePlan) has status StatusImplementing. Returns the conflicting plan filename.
180: func (ps *TaskState) HasRunningCoderInTopic(topic, excludePlan string) (bool, string) {
181: 	if topic == "" {
182: 		return false, ""
183: 	}
184: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
185: 		if filename == excludePlan {
186: 			continue
187: 		}
188: 		if entry.Topic == topic && entry.Status == StatusImplementing {
189: 			return true, filename
190: 		}
191: 	}
192: 	return false, ""
193: }
194: 
195: // Unfinished returns plans that are not done or cancelled, sorted by filename.
196: func (ps *TaskState) Unfinished() []TaskInfo {
197: 	result := make([]TaskInfo, 0, len(ps.Plans))
198: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
199: 		if entry.Status == StatusDone || entry.Status == StatusCancelled {
200: 			continue
201: 		}
202: 		result = append(result, TaskInfo{
203: 			Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
204: 			Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
205: 			Topic: entry.Topic, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
206: 		})
207: 	}
208: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
209: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
210: 	})
211: 	return result
212: }
213: 
214: // Finished returns plans that are done, sorted by creation time (newest first).
215: func (ps *TaskState) Finished() []TaskInfo {
216: 	result := make([]TaskInfo, 0)
217: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
218: 		if entry.Status != StatusDone {
219: 			continue
220: 		}
221: 		result = append(result, TaskInfo{
222: 			Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
223: 			Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
224: 			Topic: entry.Topic, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
225: 		})
226: 	}
227: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
228: 		if !result[i].CreatedAt.Equal(result[j].CreatedAt) {
229: 			return result[i].CreatedAt.After(result[j].CreatedAt)
230: 		}
231: 		return result[i].Filename > result[j].Filename
232: 	})
233: 	return result
234: }
235: 
236: // Cancelled returns all cancelled plans, sorted by filename.
237: func (ps *TaskState) Cancelled() []TaskInfo {
238: 	result := make([]TaskInfo, 0)
239: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
240: 		if entry.Status != StatusCancelled {
241: 			continue
242: 		}
243: 		result = append(result, TaskInfo{
244: 			Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
245: 			Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
246: 			Topic: entry.Topic, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
247: 		})
248: 	}
249: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
250: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
251: 	})
252: 	return result
253: }
254: 
255: // List returns all plans (including done and cancelled), sorted by filename.
256: func (ps *TaskState) List() []TaskInfo {
257: 	result := make([]TaskInfo, 0, len(ps.Plans))
258: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
259: 		result = append(result, TaskInfo{
260: 			Filename:    filename,
261: 			Status:      entry.Status,
262: 			Description: entry.Description,
263: 			Branch:      entry.Branch,
264: 			Topic:       entry.Topic,
265: 			CreatedAt:   entry.CreatedAt,
266: 		})
267: 	}
268: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
269: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
270: 	})
271: 	return result
272: }
273: 
274: // IsDone returns true only if the given plan has status StatusDone.
275: func (ps *TaskState) IsDone(filename string) bool {
276: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
277: 	if !ok {
278: 		return false
279: 	}
280: 	return entry.Status == StatusDone
281: }
282: 
283: // ForceSetStatus overrides a plan's status regardless of FSM rules.
284: // Validates the status is a known value. Use only for manual overrides (e.g. kq plan set-status --force).
285: func (ps *TaskState) ForceSetStatus(filename string, status Status) error {
286: 	if !isValidStatus(status) {
287: 		return fmt.Errorf("invalid status %q: must be one of ready, planning, implementing, reviewing, done, cancelled", status)
288: 	}
289: 	if _, ok := ps.Plans[filename]; !ok {
290: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", filename)
291: 	}
292: 	entry := ps.Plans[filename]
293: 	entry.Status = status
294: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
295: 	if err := ps.store.Update(ps.project, filename, ps.toTaskstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
296: 		return fmt.Errorf("task store: %w", err)
297: 	}
298: 	return nil
299: }
300: 
301: // isValidStatus returns true if s is a recognised lifecycle status.
302: func isValidStatus(s Status) bool {
303: 	switch s {
304: 	case StatusReady, StatusPlanning, StatusImplementing, StatusReviewing, StatusDone, StatusCancelled:
305: 		return true
306: 	}
307: 	return false
308: }
309: 
310: // setStatus updates a plan's status and persists to the store.
311: // Unexported: only for use within this package (tests). Production code must use taskfsm.Transition.
312: func (ps *TaskState) setStatus(filename string, status Status) error {
313: 	if ps.Plans == nil {
314: 		ps.Plans = make(map[string]TaskEntry)
315: 	}
316: 	entry := ps.Plans[filename]
317: 	entry.Status = status
318: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
319: 	if err := ps.store.Update(ps.project, filename, ps.toTaskstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
320: 		return fmt.Errorf("task store: %w", err)
321: 	}
322: 	return nil
323: }
324: 
325: // CreateWithContent adds a new plan entry with markdown content stored in the backend.
326: // The plan entry is created with StatusReady, and the content is persisted via store.SetContent.
327: // Returns an error if the plan already exists.
328: func (ps *TaskState) CreateWithContent(filename, description, branch, topic string, createdAt time.Time, content string) error {
329: 	if err := ps.Create(filename, description, branch, topic, createdAt); err != nil {
330: 		return err
331: 	}
332: 	if err := ps.store.SetContent(ps.project, filename, content); err != nil {
333: 		return fmt.Errorf("task store set content: %w", err)
334: 	}
335: 	return nil
336: }
337: 
338: // GetContent retrieves the markdown content for the given plan filename from the store.
339: func (ps *TaskState) GetContent(filename string) (string, error) {
340: 	return ps.store.GetContent(ps.project, filename)
341: }
342: 
343: // SetContent updates the markdown content for the given plan filename in the store.
344: func (ps *TaskState) SetContent(filename, content string) error {
345: 	return ps.store.SetContent(ps.project, filename, content)
346: }
347: 
348: // Create adds a new plan entry to the state and auto-creates the topic if needed.
349: func (ps *TaskState) Create(filename, description, branch, topic string, createdAt time.Time) error {
350: 	if ps.Plans == nil {
351: 		ps.Plans = make(map[string]TaskEntry)
352: 	}
353: 	if _, exists := ps.Plans[filename]; exists {
354: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan already exists: %s", filename)
355: 	}
356: 	entry := TaskEntry{
357: 		Status:      StatusReady,
358: 		Description: description,
359: 		Branch:      branch,
360: 		Topic:       topic,
361: 		CreatedAt:   createdAt.UTC(),
362: 	}
363: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
364: 	// Auto-create topic entry if it doesn't exist
365: 	if topic != "" {
366: 		if ps.TopicEntries == nil {
367: 			ps.TopicEntries = make(map[string]TopicEntry)
368: 		}
369: 		if _, exists := ps.TopicEntries[topic]; !exists {
370: 			ps.TopicEntries[topic] = TopicEntry{CreatedAt: createdAt.UTC()}
371: 		}
372: 	}
373: 	if err := ps.store.Create(ps.project, ps.toTaskstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
374: 		return fmt.Errorf("task store: %w", err)
375: 	}
376: 	// Auto-create topic in store if needed
377: 	if topic != "" {
378: 		topicEntry := taskstore.TopicEntry{Name: topic, CreatedAt: createdAt.UTC()}
379: 		if err := ps.store.CreateTopic(ps.project, topicEntry); err != nil {
380: 			// Ignore "already exists" errors for topics
381: 			if !isAlreadyExistsError(err) {
382: 				return fmt.Errorf("task store: %w", err)
383: 			}
384: 		}
385: 	}
386: 	return nil
387: }
388: 
389: // Register adds a new plan entry with metadata and persists to the store.
390: // Returns an error if the plan already exists.
391: func (ps *TaskState) Register(filename, description, branch string, createdAt time.Time) error {
392: 	if ps.Plans == nil {
393: 		ps.Plans = make(map[string]TaskEntry)
394: 	}
395: 	if _, exists := ps.Plans[filename]; exists {
396: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan already exists: %s", filename)
397: 	}
398: 	entry := TaskEntry{
399: 		Status:      StatusReady,
400: 		Description: description,
401: 		Branch:      branch,
402: 		CreatedAt:   createdAt.UTC(),
403: 	}
404: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
405: 	if err := ps.store.Create(ps.project, ps.toTaskstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
406: 		return fmt.Errorf("task store: %w", err)
407: 	}
408: 	return nil
409: }
410: 
411: // Entry returns the TaskEntry for the given filename, and whether it exists.
412: func (ps *TaskState) Entry(filename string) (TaskEntry, bool) {
413: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
414: 	return entry, ok
415: }
416: 
417: // SetTopic assigns a topic to an existing plan entry and persists to the store.
418: // If topic is non-empty and does not yet exist in TopicEntries, it is auto-created.
419: // Pass an empty string to remove the plan from any topic.
420: func (ps *TaskState) SetTopic(filename, topic string) error {
421: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
422: 	if !ok {
423: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", filename)
424: 	}
425: 	entry.Topic = topic
426: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
427: 	// Auto-create topic entry if it doesn't exist
428: 	if topic != "" {
429: 		if ps.TopicEntries == nil {
430: 			ps.TopicEntries = make(map[string]TopicEntry)
431: 		}
432: 		if _, exists := ps.TopicEntries[topic]; !exists {
433: 			ps.TopicEntries[topic] = TopicEntry{CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC()}
434: 		}
435: 	}
436: 	if err := ps.store.Update(ps.project, filename, ps.toTaskstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
437: 		return fmt.Errorf("task store: %w", err)
438: 	}
439: 	// Auto-create topic in store if needed
440: 	if topic != "" {
441: 		topicEntry := taskstore.TopicEntry{Name: topic, CreatedAt: ps.TopicEntries[topic].CreatedAt}
442: 		if err := ps.store.CreateTopic(ps.project, topicEntry); err != nil {
443: 			if !isAlreadyExistsError(err) {
444: 				return fmt.Errorf("task store: %w", err)
445: 			}
446: 		}
447: 	}
448: 	return nil
449: }
450: 
451: // SetBranch assigns a branch name to an existing plan entry and persists to the store.
452: func (ps *TaskState) SetBranch(filename, branch string) error {
453: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
454: 	if !ok {
455: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", filename)
456: 	}
457: 	entry.Branch = branch
458: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
459: 	if err := ps.store.Update(ps.project, filename, ps.toTaskstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
460: 		return fmt.Errorf("task store: %w", err)
461: 	}
462: 	return nil
463: }
464: 
465: // Save is a no-op — all mutations write through to the store immediately.
466: // Retained for API compatibility.
467: func (ps *TaskState) Save() error {
468: 	return nil
469: }
470: 
471: // DisplayName strips the .md extension from a plan filename.
472: // "auth-refactor.md" → "auth-refactor"
473: func DisplayName(filename string) string {
474: 	return strings.TrimSuffix(filename, ".md")
475: }
476: 
477: // Rename renames a plan by giving it a new display name slug.
478: // It renames the .md file on disk (if it exists), rekeys the planstate entry,
479: // and persists the updated state.
480: // newName should be a human-readable name (e.g., "auth refactor") which will be
481: // slugified automatically. Returns the new filename on success.
482: func (ps *TaskState) Rename(oldFilename, newName string) (string, error) {
483: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[oldFilename]
484: 	if !ok {
485: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", oldFilename)
486: 	}
487: 	if newName == "" {
488: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("new name cannot be empty")
489: 	}
490: 
491: 	// Build new filename from the slug alone.
492: 	newSlug := slugify(newName)
493: 	if newSlug == "" {
494: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("new name produced an empty slug")
495: 	}
496: 	newFilename := newSlug + ".md"
497: 
498: 	if newFilename == oldFilename {
499: 		return oldFilename, nil // nothing to do
500: 	}
501: 	if _, exists := ps.Plans[newFilename]; exists {
502: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("a plan named %q already exists", newFilename)
503: 	}
504: 
505: 	// Rename file on disk if it exists.
506: 	oldPath := filepath.Join(ps.Dir, oldFilename)
507: 	newPath := filepath.Join(ps.Dir, newFilename)
508: 	if _, err := os.Stat(oldPath); err == nil {
509: 		if err := os.Rename(oldPath, newPath); err != nil {
510: 			return "", fmt.Errorf("rename plan file: %w", err)
511: 		}
512: 	}
513: 
514: 	// Rekey the planstate entry.
515: 	ps.Plans[newFilename] = entry
516: 	delete(ps.Plans, oldFilename)
517: 
518: 	if err := ps.store.Rename(ps.project, oldFilename, newFilename); err != nil {
519: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("task store: %w", err)
520: 	}
521: 	return newFilename, nil
522: }
523: 
524: // slugify converts a human name to a lowercase, hyphen-separated slug.
525: // "My Cool Feature!" → "my-cool-feature"
526: func slugify(name string) string {
527: 	name = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(name))
528: 	// Replace any sequence of non-alphanumeric characters with a hyphen.
529: 	result := make([]rune, 0, len(name))
530: 	inHyphen := false
531: 	for _, r := range name {
532: 		if r >= 'a' && r <= 'z' || r >= '0' && r <= '9' {
533: 			result = append(result, r)
534: 			inHyphen = false
535: 		} else if !inHyphen && len(result) > 0 {
536: 			result = append(result, '-')
537: 			inHyphen = true
538: 		}
539: 	}
540: 	// Trim trailing hyphen.
541: 	for len(result) > 0 && result[len(result)-1] == '-' {
542: 		result = result[:len(result)-1]
543: 	}
544: 	return string(result)
545: }
546: 
547: // isAlreadyExistsError returns true if the error indicates a duplicate resource.
548: func isAlreadyExistsError(err error) bool {
549: 	if err == nil {
550: 		return false
551: 	}
552: 	msg := err.Error()
553: 	return strings.Contains(msg, "already exists")
554: }
555: 
556: // toTaskstoreEntry converts a local TaskEntry to a taskstore.TaskEntry for
557: // writing to the store.
558: func (ps *TaskState) toTaskstoreEntry(filename string, e TaskEntry) taskstore.TaskEntry {
559: 	return taskstore.TaskEntry{
560: 		Filename:      filename,
561: 		Status:        taskstore.Status(e.Status),
562: 		Description:   e.Description,
563: 		Branch:        e.Branch,
564: 		Topic:         e.Topic,
565: 		CreatedAt:     e.CreatedAt,
566: 		Implemented:   e.Implemented,
567: 		ClickUpTaskID: e.ClickUpTaskID,
568: 		ReviewCycle:   e.ReviewCycle,
569: 	}
570: }
571: 
572: // SetClickUpTaskID assigns a ClickUp task ID to an existing plan entry and
573: // persists to the store.
574: func (ps *TaskState) SetClickUpTaskID(filename, taskID string) error {
575: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
576: 	if !ok {
577: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", filename)
578: 	}
579: 	entry.ClickUpTaskID = taskID
580: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
581: 	if err := ps.store.SetClickUpTaskID(ps.project, filename, taskID); err != nil {
582: 		return fmt.Errorf("task store: %w", err)
583: 	}
584: 	return nil
585: }
586: 
587: // ReviewCycle returns the current review cycle counter for the given plan.
588: // Returns an error if the plan is not found.
589: func (ps *TaskState) ReviewCycle(filename string) (int, error) {
590: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
591: 	if !ok {
592: 		return 0, fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", filename)
593: 	}
594: 	return entry.ReviewCycle, nil
595: }
596: 
597: // IncrementReviewCycle increments the review cycle counter for the given plan
598: // and persists to the store. Updates the in-memory state to reflect the new value.
599: // Returns an error if the plan is not found.
600: func (ps *TaskState) IncrementReviewCycle(filename string) error {
601: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
602: 	if !ok {
603: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", filename)
604: 	}
605: 	if err := ps.store.IncrementReviewCycle(ps.project, filename); err != nil {
606: 		return fmt.Errorf("task store: %w", err)
607: 	}
608: 	entry.ReviewCycle++
609: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
610: 	return nil
611: }

(End of file - total 611 lines)
</content>

<content>1: package git
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 	"path/filepath"
6: 	"strings"
7: 
8: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config/taskstate"
9: )
10: 
11: // TaskBranchFromFile derives the git branch name from a plan filename.
12: // "auth-refactor.md" → "plan/auth-refactor"
13: func TaskBranchFromFile(planFile string) string {
14: 	name := taskstate.DisplayName(planFile)
15: 	name = sanitizeBranchName(name)
16: 	if name == "" {
17: 		name = "plan"
18: 	}
19: 	return "plan/" + name
20: }
21: 
22: // TaskWorktreePath returns the worktree path for a plan branch.
23: // The branch separator "/" is replaced with "-" to form a valid directory name.
24: func TaskWorktreePath(repoPath, branch string) string {
25: 	safe := strings.ReplaceAll(branch, "/", "-")
26: 	return filepath.Join(repoPath, ".worktrees", safe)
27: }
28: 
29: // NewSharedTaskWorktree constructs a GitWorktree for the shared plan worktree
30: // (used by coder and reviewer sessions that share the same branch).
31: func NewSharedTaskWorktree(repoPath, branch string) *GitWorktree {
32: 	return NewGitWorktreeFromStorage(
33: 		repoPath,
34: 		TaskWorktreePath(repoPath, branch),
35: 		"plan-shared",
36: 		branch,
37: 		"",
38: 	)
39: }
40: 
41: // CommitTaskScaffoldOnMain stages the plan stub file and plan-state.json on the
42: // main branch (repoPath is the main worktree) and creates a commit.
43: func CommitTaskScaffoldOnMain(repoPath, planFile string) error {
44: 	gt := &GitWorktree{repoPath: repoPath, worktreePath: repoPath}
45: 	planPath := filepath.Join("docs", "plans", planFile)
46: 	statePath := filepath.Join("docs", "plans", "plan-state.json")
47: 	if _, err := gt.runGitCommand(repoPath, "add", planPath, statePath); err != nil {
48: 		return fmt.Errorf("stage plan scaffold: %w", err)
49: 	}
50: 	if _, err := gt.runGitCommand(repoPath, "commit", "-m", "feat(plan): add "+taskstate.DisplayName(planFile)+" scaffold"); err != nil {
51: 		if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "nothing to commit") {
52: 			return nil
53: 		}
54: 		return fmt.Errorf("commit plan scaffold: %w", err)
55: 	}
56: 	return nil
57: }
58: 
59: // EnsureTaskBranch creates the plan branch off the current HEAD if it doesn't
60: // already exist. It is idempotent.
61: func EnsureTaskBranch(repoPath, branch string) error {
62: 	gt := &GitWorktree{repoPath: repoPath, worktreePath: repoPath}
63: 	if _, err := gt.runGitCommand(repoPath, "rev-parse", "--verify", branch); err == nil {
64: 		return nil // already exists
65: 	}
66: 	if _, err := gt.runGitCommand(repoPath, "branch", branch); err != nil {
67: 		return fmt.Errorf("create plan branch %s: %w", branch, err)
68: 	}
69: 	return nil
70: }
71: 
72: // MergeTaskBranch merges the plan branch into the current branch (typically main),
73: // removes the worktree, and deletes the plan branch.
74: func MergeTaskBranch(repoPath, branch string) error {
75: 	gt := &GitWorktree{repoPath: repoPath, worktreePath: repoPath}
76: 	worktreePath := TaskWorktreePath(repoPath, branch)
77: 
78: 	// Remove worktree first so the branch isn't "checked out" elsewhere.
79: 	_, _ = gt.runGitCommand(repoPath, "worktree", "remove", "-f", worktreePath)
80: 	_, _ = gt.runGitCommand(repoPath, "worktree", "prune")
81: 
82: 	// Ensure the local branch exists — worktree removal may have deleted it.
83: 	// If a remote tracking branch exists, recreate the local one from it.
84: 	if _, err := gt.runGitCommand(repoPath, "rev-parse", "--verify", branch); err != nil {
85: 		remote := "origin/" + branch
86: 		if _, remoteErr := gt.runGitCommand(repoPath, "rev-parse", "--verify", remote); remoteErr == nil {
87: 			if _, brErr := gt.runGitCommand(repoPath, "branch", branch, remote); brErr != nil {
88: 				return fmt.Errorf("recreate local branch %s from remote: %w", branch, brErr)
89: 			}
90: 		} else {
91: 			return fmt.Errorf("branch %s not found locally or on remote", branch)
92: 		}
93: 	}
94: 
95: 	// Merge the plan branch into the current branch.
96: 	if _, err := gt.runGitCommand(repoPath, "merge", branch, "--no-ff", "-m",
97: 		fmt.Sprintf("merge plan branch %s", branch)); err != nil {
98: 		return fmt.Errorf("merge %s: %w", branch, err)
99: 	}
100: 
101: 	// Delete the plan branch after successful merge.
102: 	if _, err := gt.runGitCommand(repoPath, "branch", "-d", branch); err != nil {
103: 		// Non-fatal: merge succeeded, branch cleanup is best-effort.
104: 		_ = err
105: 	}
106: 
107: 	return nil
108: }
109: 
110: // ResetTaskBranch removes the plan worktree (if any), deletes the branch, and
111: // recreates it from the current HEAD. Used by "start over".
112: func ResetTaskBranch(repoPath, branch string) error {
113: 	gt := &GitWorktree{repoPath: repoPath, worktreePath: repoPath}
114: 	worktreePath := TaskWorktreePath(repoPath, branch)
115: 	_, _ = gt.runGitCommand(repoPath, "worktree", "remove", "-f", worktreePath)
116: 	_, _ = gt.runGitCommand(repoPath, "branch", "-D", branch)
117: 	if _, err := gt.runGitCommand(repoPath, "branch", branch); err != nil {
118: 		return fmt.Errorf("recreate plan branch %s: %w", branch, err)
119: 	}
120: 	if _, err := gt.runGitCommand(repoPath, "worktree", "prune"); err != nil {
121: 		return fmt.Errorf("prune worktrees: %w", err)
122: 	}
123: 	return nil
124: }

<content>1: package git
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"errors"
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"os"
7: 	"os/exec"
8: 	"path/filepath"
9: 	"strings"
10: 
11: 	"github.com/go-git/go-git/v5"
12: 	"github.com/go-git/go-git/v5/plumbing"
13: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/log"
14: )
15: 
16: // Setup creates a new worktree for the session. It creates the worktrees
17: // directory and determines whether the target branch already exists,
18: // dispatching to the appropriate setup path.
19: func (g *GitWorktree) Setup() error {
20: 	wtDir, err := getWorktreeDirectory(g.repoPath)
21: 	if err != nil {
22: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get worktree directory: %w", err)
23: 	}
24: 
25: 	type result struct{ err error }
26: 	mkdirDone := make(chan result, 1)
27: 	branchCheckDone := make(chan result, 1)
28: 	var branchFound bool
29: 
30: 	go func() {
31: 		mkdirDone <- result{os.MkdirAll(wtDir, 0755)}
32: 	}()
33: 
34: 	go func() {
35: 		repo, openErr := git.PlainOpen(g.repoPath)
36: 		if openErr != nil {
37: 			branchCheckDone <- result{fmt.Errorf("failed to open repository: %w", openErr)}
38: 			return
39: 		}
40: 		ref := plumbing.NewBranchReferenceName(g.branchName)
41: 		if _, refErr := repo.Reference(ref, false); refErr == nil {
42: 			branchFound = true
43: 		}
44: 		branchCheckDone <- result{nil}
45: 	}()
46: 
47: 	if r := <-mkdirDone; r.err != nil {
48: 		return r.err
49: 	}
50: 	if r := <-branchCheckDone; r.err != nil {
51: 		return r.err
52: 	}
53: 
54: 	if branchFound {
55: 		return g.setupFromExistingBranch()
56: 	}
57: 	return g.setupNewWorktree()
58: }
59: 
60: // setupFromExistingBranch creates a worktree from a branch that already exists
61: // in the repository. It first removes any stale worktree at the target path,
62: // syncs the local branch with the remote, then creates the fresh worktree and
63: // resolves the base commit SHA for diff computation.
64: func (g *GitWorktree) setupFromExistingBranch() error {
65: 	// Remove any stale worktree; ignore errors (it may not exist).
66: 	_, _ = g.runGitCommand(g.repoPath, "worktree", "remove", "-f", g.worktreePath)
67: 
68: 	// Best-effort sync with remote before creating the worktree.
69: 	g.syncBranchWithRemote()
70: 
71: 	if _, err := g.runGitCommand(g.repoPath, "worktree", "add", g.worktreePath, g.branchName); err != nil {
72: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create worktree from branch %s: %w", g.branchName, err)
73: 	}
74: 
75: 	// Resolve the base commit SHA for diff computation.
76: 	if g.baseCommitSHA == "" {
77: 		if out, err := g.runGitCommand(g.repoPath, "merge-base", "HEAD", g.branchName); err == nil {
78: 			g.baseCommitSHA = strings.TrimSpace(out)
79: 		} else if out, err := g.runGitCommand(g.worktreePath, "rev-parse", "HEAD"); err == nil {
80: 			g.baseCommitSHA = strings.TrimSpace(out)
81: 		}
82: 	}
83: 
84: 	return nil
85: }
86: 
87: // syncBranchWithRemote reconciles the local branch with its remote counterpart.
88: // It fetches origin/<branch>, and if the SHAs differ it either fast-forwards
89: // (when local is an ancestor of remote) or rebases local commits onto remote
90: // (when the histories have diverged). All errors are logged but not propagated —
91: // this is a best-effort operation.
92: func (g *GitWorktree) syncBranchWithRemote() {
93: 	remote := "origin/" + g.branchName
94: 
95: 	_, _ = g.runGitCommand(g.repoPath, "fetch", "origin", g.branchName)
96: 
97: 	// Bail early if the remote tracking branch does not exist.
98: 	if _, err := g.runGitCommand(g.repoPath, "rev-parse", "--verify", remote); err != nil {
99: 		return
100: 	}
101: 
102: 	localOut, err := g.runGitCommand(g.repoPath, "rev-parse", g.branchName)
103: 	if err != nil {
104: 		return
105: 	}
106: 	remoteOut, err := g.runGitCommand(g.repoPath, "rev-parse", remote)
107: 	if err != nil {
108: 		return
109: 	}
110: 
111: 	if strings.TrimSpace(localOut) == strings.TrimSpace(remoteOut) {
112: 		return // already in sync
113: 	}
114: 
115: 	// Fast-forward if local is an ancestor of remote.
116: 	if _, err := g.runGitCommand(g.repoPath, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", g.branchName, remote); err == nil {
117: 		if _, ffErr := g.runGitCommand(g.repoPath, "branch", "-f", g.branchName, remote); ffErr != nil {
118: 			log.WarningLog.Printf("syncBranchWithRemote: fast-forward %s to %s failed: %v", g.branchName, remote, ffErr)
119: 		}
120: 		return
121: 	}
122: 
123: 	// Histories have diverged — do NOT rebase from the main worktree.
124: 	// Running `git rebase ... <branch>` checks out <branch> in the current
125: 	// worktree as a side effect, which would poison the main worktree by
126: 	// switching it away from its own branch. Instead, leave the branch as-is
127: 	// and let the caller handle the divergence after the worktree is created.
128: 	log.WarningLog.Printf(
129: 		"syncBranchWithRemote: %s has diverged from %s — leaving as-is to avoid poisoning the main worktree",
130: 		g.branchName, remote,
131: 	)
132: }
133: 
134: // setupNewWorktree creates a brand-new branch from the current HEAD and adds
135: // a worktree for it. It errors early if the repository has no commits yet.
136: func (g *GitWorktree) setupNewWorktree() error {
137: 	// Remove any stale worktree; ignore errors.
138: 	_, _ = g.runGitCommand(g.repoPath, "worktree", "remove", "-f", g.worktreePath)
139: 
140: 	repo, err := git.PlainOpen(g.repoPath)
141: 	if err != nil {
142: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to open repository: %w", err)
143: 	}
144: 
145: 	if err := g.cleanupExistingBranch(repo); err != nil {
146: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to cleanup existing branch: %w", err)
147: 	}
148: 
149: 	headOut, err := g.runGitCommand(g.repoPath, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
150: 	if err != nil {
151: 		msg := err.Error()
152: 		if strings.Contains(msg, "fatal: ambiguous argument 'HEAD'") ||
153: 			strings.Contains(msg, "fatal: not a valid object name") ||
154: 			strings.Contains(msg, "fatal: HEAD: not a valid object name") {
155: 			return fmt.Errorf("this appears to be a brand new repository: please create an initial commit before creating an instance")
156: 		}
157: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get HEAD commit hash: %w", err)
158: 	}
159: 	headCommit := strings.TrimSpace(headOut)
160: 	g.baseCommitSHA = headCommit
161: 
162: 	if _, err := g.runGitCommand(g.repoPath, "worktree", "add", "-b", g.branchName, g.worktreePath, headCommit); err != nil {
163: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create worktree from commit %s: %w", headCommit, err)
164: 	}
165: 
166: 	return nil
167: }
168: 
169: // Cleanup removes the worktree and its associated branch, then prunes. All
170: // sub-errors are collected and returned together via errors.Join.
171: func (g *GitWorktree) Cleanup() error {
172: 	var errs []error
173: 
174: 	if _, statErr := os.Stat(g.worktreePath); statErr == nil {
175: 		if _, rmErr := g.runGitCommand(g.repoPath, "worktree", "remove", "-f", g.worktreePath); rmErr != nil {
176: 			errs = append(errs, rmErr)
177: 		}
178: 	} else if !os.IsNotExist(statErr) {
179: 		errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("failed to stat worktree path: %w", statErr))
180: 	}
181: 
182: 	repo, err := git.PlainOpen(g.repoPath)
183: 	if err != nil {
184: 		errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("failed to open repository for cleanup: %w", err))
185: 		return errors.Join(errs...)
186: 	}
187: 
188: 	branchRef := plumbing.NewBranchReferenceName(g.branchName)
189: 	if _, err := repo.Reference(branchRef, false); err == nil {
190: 		if removeErr := repo.Storer.RemoveReference(branchRef); removeErr != nil {
191: 			errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("failed to remove branch %s: %w", g.branchName, removeErr))
192: 		}
193: 	} else if err != plumbing.ErrReferenceNotFound {
194: 		errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("error checking branch %s: %w", g.branchName, err))
195: 	}
196: 
197: 	if pruneErr := g.Prune(); pruneErr != nil {
198: 		errs = append(errs, pruneErr)
199: 	}
200: 
201: 	return errors.Join(errs...)
202: }
203: 
204: // Remove removes the worktree filesystem entry and git metadata but leaves the
205: // branch intact so it can be re-attached later.
206: func (g *GitWorktree) Remove() error {
207: 	if _, err := g.runGitCommand(g.repoPath, "worktree", "remove", "-f", g.worktreePath); err != nil {
208: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to remove worktree: %w", err)
209: 	}
210: 	return nil
211: }
212: 
213: // Prune runs `git worktree prune` to remove stale administrative files.
214: func (g *GitWorktree) Prune() error {
215: 	if _, err := g.runGitCommand(g.repoPath, "worktree", "prune"); err != nil {
216: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to prune worktrees: %w", err)
217: 	}
218: 	return nil
219: }
220: 
221: // CleanupWorktrees removes every worktree found under repoPath/.worktrees/ and
222: // deletes the associated branch for each one, then prunes. Worktrees that
223: // cannot be removed via git fall back to os.RemoveAll.
224: func CleanupWorktrees(repoPath string) error {
225: 	wtDir, err := getWorktreeDirectory(repoPath)
226: 	if err != nil {
227: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get worktree directory: %w", err)
228: 	}
229: 
230: 	entries, err := os.ReadDir(wtDir)
231: 	if err != nil {
232: 		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
233: 			return nil
234: 		}
235: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to read worktree directory: %w", err)
236: 	}
237: 
238: 	run := func(args ...string) (string, error) {
239: 		cmd := exec.Command("git", append([]string{"-C", repoPath}, args...)...)
240: 		out, cmdErr := cmd.CombinedOutput()
241: 		if cmdErr != nil {
242: 			return "", fmt.Errorf("git %v: %s (%w)", args, out, cmdErr)
243: 		}
244: 		return string(out), nil
245: 	}
246: 
247: 	// Build a path→branch map from the porcelain worktree list.
248: 	listOut, err := run("worktree", "list", "--porcelain")
249: 	if err != nil {
250: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to list worktrees: %w", err)
251: 	}
252: 
253: 	pathToBranch := make(map[string]string)
254: 	var currentPath string
255: 	for _, line := range strings.Split(listOut, "\n") {
256: 		switch {
257: 		case strings.HasPrefix(line, "worktree "):
258: 			currentPath = strings.TrimPrefix(line, "worktree ")
259: 		case strings.HasPrefix(line, "branch "):
260: 			branch := strings.TrimPrefix(line, "branch ")
261: 			branch = strings.TrimPrefix(branch, "refs/heads/")
262: 			if currentPath != "" {
263: 				pathToBranch[currentPath] = branch
264: 			}
265: 		}
266: 	}
267: 
268: 	for _, entry := range entries {
269: 		if !entry.IsDir() {
270: 			continue
271: 		}
272: 
273: 		wtPath := filepath.Join(wtDir, entry.Name())
274: 
275: 		if _, rmErr := run("worktree", "remove", "-f", wtPath); rmErr != nil {
276: 			log.WarningLog.Printf("git worktree remove failed for %s, falling back to os.RemoveAll: %v", wtPath, rmErr)
277: 			if fsErr := os.RemoveAll(wtPath); fsErr != nil {
278: 				log.ErrorLog.Printf("failed to remove worktree path %s: %v", wtPath, fsErr)
279: 			}
280: 		}
281: 
282: 		// Delete the branch that was associated with this worktree path.
283: 		for p, branch := range pathToBranch {
284: 			if strings.Contains(p, entry.Name()) {
285: 				if _, delErr := run("branch", "-D", branch); delErr != nil {
286: 					log.ErrorLog.Printf("failed to delete branch %s: %v", branch, delErr)
287: 				}
288: 				break
289: 			}
290: 		}
291: 	}
292: 
293: 	if _, pruneErr := run("worktree", "prune"); pruneErr != nil {
294: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to prune worktrees: %w", pruneErr)
295: 	}
296: 
297: 	return nil
298: }

(End of file - total 298 lines)
</content>

<content>1: package git
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 	"os/exec"
6: 	"strings"
7: 
8: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/log"
9: )
10: 
11: // runGitCommand executes a git command with the given path as the working directory.
12: // It builds the invocation as: git -C <path> <args...>, captures combined stdout+stderr,
13: // and wraps any error with the captured output for context.
14: func (g *GitWorktree) runGitCommand(path string, args ...string) (string, error) {
15: 	cmdArgs := append([]string{"-C", path}, args...)
16: 	cmd := exec.Command("git", cmdArgs...)
17: 	out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
18: 	if err != nil {
19: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("git command failed: %s (%w)", out, err)
20: 	}
21: 	return string(out), nil
22: }
23: 
24: // PushChanges stages, commits, and pushes the worktree's current state to the remote.
25: // It requires the GitHub CLI (gh) to be present and authenticated.
26: // When open is true the branch URL is opened in the browser after a successful push.
27: func (g *GitWorktree) PushChanges(commitMessage string, open bool) error {
28: 	if err := checkGHCLI(); err != nil {
29: 		return err
30: 	}
31: 	if err := g.CommitChanges(commitMessage); err != nil {
32: 		return err
33: 	}
34: 	return g.Push(open)
35: }
36: 
37: // Push pushes the current branch to origin without committing first.
38: // If open is true it attempts to open the remote branch URL; any error from
39: // that step is logged but not returned.
40: func (g *GitWorktree) Push(open bool) error {
41: 	if _, err := g.runGitCommand(g.worktreePath, "push", "-u", "origin", g.branchName); err != nil {
42: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to push branch %s: %w", g.branchName, err)
43: 	}
44: 	if open {
45: 		if err := g.OpenBranchURL(); err != nil {
46: 			log.ErrorLog.Printf("failed to open branch URL: %v", err)
47: 		}
48: 	}
49: 	return nil
50: }
51: 
52: // GeneratePRBody builds a markdown pull-request description that summarises
53: // the files changed, the commit history, and diff statistics since baseCommitSHA.
54: // It returns an error if no base commit SHA is available.
55: func (g *GitWorktree) GeneratePRBody() (string, error) {
56: 	base := g.GetBaseCommitSHA()
57: 	if base == "" {
58: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("no base commit SHA available")
59: 	}
60: 
61: 	var sections []string
62: 
63: 	// List of files that changed relative to the base commit.
64: 	if files, err := g.runGitCommand(g.worktreePath, "diff", "--name-only", base); err == nil {
65: 		if trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(files); trimmed != "" {
66: 			sections = append(sections, "## Changes\n\n"+trimmed)
67: 		}
68: 	}
69: 
70: 	// One-line commit log from base to HEAD.
71: 	if commits, err := g.runGitCommand(g.worktreePath, "log", "--oneline", base+"..HEAD"); err == nil {
72: 		if trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(commits); trimmed != "" {
73: 			sections = append(sections, "## Commits\n\n"+trimmed)
74: 		}
75: 	}
76: 
77: 	// Summary statistics of insertions/deletions.
78: 	if stats, err := g.runGitCommand(g.worktreePath, "diff", "--stat", base); err == nil {
79: 		if trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(stats); trimmed != "" {
80: 			sections = append(sections, "## Stats\n\n"+trimmed)
81: 		}
82: 	}
83: 
84: 	if len(sections) == 0 {
85: 		return "", nil
86: 	}
87: 	return strings.Join(sections, "\n\n"), nil
88: }
89: 
90: // CreatePR pushes the current branch and opens a pull request on GitHub.
91: // If the PR already exists it opens the existing one in the browser instead.
92: func (g *GitWorktree) CreatePR(title, body, commitMsg string) error {
93: 	if err := g.PushChanges(commitMsg, false); err != nil {
94: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to push changes: %w", err)
95: 	}
96: 
97: 	prCmd := exec.Command("gh", "pr", "create", "--title", title, "--body", body, "--head", g.branchName)
98: 	prCmd.Dir = g.worktreePath
99: 	out, err := prCmd.CombinedOutput()
100: 	if err != nil {
101: 		if strings.Contains(string(out), "already exists") {
102: 			viewCmd := exec.Command("gh", "pr", "view", "--web", g.branchName)
103: 			viewCmd.Dir = g.worktreePath
104: 			_ = viewCmd.Run()
105: 			return nil
106: 		}
107: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create PR: %s (%w)", out, err)
108: 	}
109: 
110: 	viewCmd := exec.Command("gh", "pr", "view", "--web", g.branchName)
111: 	viewCmd.Dir = g.worktreePath
112: 	_ = viewCmd.Run()
113: 	return nil
114: }
115: 
116: // CommitChanges stages all changes and creates a commit with the given message.
117: // It is a no-op when the worktree is clean.
118: func (g *GitWorktree) CommitChanges(commitMessage string) error {
119: 	dirty, err := g.IsDirty()
120: 	if err != nil {
121: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to check for changes: %w", err)
122: 	}
123: 	if !dirty {
124: 		return nil
125: 	}
126: 	if _, err := g.runGitCommand(g.worktreePath, "add", "."); err != nil {
127: 		log.ErrorLog.Print(err)
128: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to stage changes: %w", err)
129: 	}
130: 	if _, err := g.runGitCommand(g.worktreePath, "commit", "-m", commitMessage, "--no-verify"); err != nil {
131: 		log.ErrorLog.Print(err)
132: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to commit changes: %w", err)
133: 	}
134: 	return nil
135: }
136: 
137: // IsDirty reports whether the worktree contains uncommitted changes.
138: func (g *GitWorktree) IsDirty() (bool, error) {
139: 	out, err := g.runGitCommand(g.worktreePath, "status", "--porcelain")
140: 	if err != nil {
141: 		return false, fmt.Errorf("failed to check worktree status: %w", err)
142: 	}
143: 	return len(out) > 0, nil
144: }
145: 
146: // IsBranchCheckedOut reports whether the configured branch is the currently
147: // checked-out branch in the repository (not in the worktree).
148: func (g *GitWorktree) IsBranchCheckedOut() (bool, error) {
149: 	out, err := g.runGitCommand(g.repoPath, "branch", "--show-current")
150: 	if err != nil {
151: 		return false, fmt.Errorf("failed to get current branch: %w", err)
152: 	}
153: 	return strings.TrimSpace(out) == g.branchName, nil
154: }
155: 
156: // OpenBranchURL opens the remote branch page in the default browser using the
157: // GitHub CLI. It requires gh to be present and authenticated.
158: func (g *GitWorktree) OpenBranchURL() error {
159: 	if err := checkGHCLI(); err != nil {
160: 		return err
161: 	}
162: 	cmd := exec.Command("gh", "browse", "--branch", g.branchName)
163: 	cmd.Dir = g.worktreePath
164: 	if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
165: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to open branch URL: %w", err)
166: 	}
167: 	return nil
168: }

(End of file - total 168 lines)
</content>

<content>1: package app
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 	"os/exec"
6: 
7: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config"
8: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config/auditlog"
9: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config/taskfsm"
10: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config/taskparser"
11: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config/taskstate"
12: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/internal/initcmd/scaffold"
13: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/session"
14: 	gitpkg "github.com/kastheco/kasmos/session/git"
15: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/ui/overlay"
16: 
17: 	tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
18: 	"github.com/atotto/clipboard"
19: )
20: 
21: // executeContextAction performs the action selected from a context menu.
22: func (m *home) executeContextAction(action string) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
23: 	switch action {
24: 	case "kill_instance":
25: 		selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
26: 		if selected != nil {
27: 			// Emit audit before attempting pause so the event is always recorded
28: 			// even when the instance has not been started (e.g. exited, loading).
29: 			m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentKilled, "agent stopped (branch preserved)",
30: 				auditlog.WithInstance(selected.Title),
31: 				auditlog.WithAgent(selected.AgentType),
32: 				auditlog.WithPlan(selected.TaskFile),
33: 			)
34: 			if err := selected.Pause(); err != nil {
35: 				return m, m.handleError(err)
36: 			}
37: 			m.saveAllInstances()
38: 			m.updateNavPanelStatus()
39: 		}
40: 		return m, tea.Batch(tea.RequestWindowSize, m.instanceChanged())
41: 
42: 	case "open_instance":
43: 		selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
44: 		if selected == nil || !selected.Started() || selected.Paused() || !selected.TmuxAlive() {
45: 			return m, nil
46: 		}
47: 		return m, func() tea.Msg {
48: 			ch, err := m.nav.Attach()
49: 			if err != nil {
50: 				return err
51: 			}
52: 			<-ch
53: 			return instanceChangedMsg{}
54: 		}
55: 
56: 	case "pause_instance":
57: 		selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
58: 		if selected != nil && selected.Status != session.Paused {
59: 			if err := selected.Pause(); err != nil {
60: 				return m, m.handleError(err)
61: 			}
62: 			m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentPaused, "agent paused",
63: 				auditlog.WithInstance(selected.Title),
64: 				auditlog.WithAgent(selected.AgentType),
65: 				auditlog.WithPlan(selected.TaskFile),
66: 			)
67: 			m.saveAllInstances()
68: 		}
69: 		return m, tea.Batch(tea.RequestWindowSize, m.instanceChanged())
70: 
71: 	case "resume_instance":
72: 		selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
73: 		if selected != nil && selected.Status == session.Paused {
74: 			if err := selected.Resume(); err != nil {
75: 				return m, m.handleError(err)

743: // triggerTaskStage handles a user action on a plan lifecycle stage row.
744: // It checks if the stage is locked, applies the concurrency gate for the
745: // implement stage, and then executes the stage transition.
746: func (m *home) triggerTaskStage(planFile, stage string) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
747: 	if m.taskState == nil {
748: 		return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("no task state loaded"))
749: 	}
750: 	entry, ok := m.taskState.Plans[planFile]
751: 	if !ok {
752: 		return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("missing task state for %s", planFile))
753: 	}
754: 
755: 	// Backfill branch name for plans created before the branch field existed.
756: 	if entry.Branch == "" {
757: 		entry.Branch = gitpkg.TaskBranchFromFile(planFile)
758: 		if err := m.taskState.SetBranch(planFile, entry.Branch); err != nil {
759: 			return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("failed to assign branch for plan: %w", err))
760: 		}
761: 	}
762: 
763: 	// Check if stage is locked
764: 	if isLocked(entry.Status, stage) {
765: 		prev := map[string]string{
766: 			"implement": "plan",
767: 			"review":    "implement",
768: 			"finished":  "review",
769: 		}[stage]
770: 		m.toastManager.Error(fmt.Sprintf("complete '%s' first", prev))
771: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
772: 	}
773: 
774: 	// Concurrency gate for coder stages
775: 	if (stage == "implement" || stage == "solo") && entry.Topic != "" {
776: 		if hasConflict, conflictPlan := m.taskState.HasRunningCoderInTopic(entry.Topic, planFile); hasConflict {
777: 			conflictName := taskstate.DisplayName(conflictPlan)
778: 			message := fmt.Sprintf("⚠ %s is already running in topic \"%s\"\n\nrunning both plans may cause issues.\ncontinue anyway?", conflictName, entry.Topic)
779: 			proceedAction := func() tea.Msg {
780: 				return taskStageConfirmedMsg{planFile: planFile, stage: stage}
781: 			}
782: 			return m, m.confirmAction(message, proceedAction)
783: 		}
784: 	}
785: 
786: 	return m.executeTaskStage(planFile, stage)
787: }
788: 
789: // executeTaskStage runs the actual stage logic (agent spawn, wave orchestration)
790: // after all gates (lock check, concurrency) have passed. Called directly from
791: // triggerTaskStage on the normal path, and via taskStageConfirmedMsg when the
792: // user confirms past the topic-concurrency gate.
793: func (m *home) executeTaskStage(planFile, stage string) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
794: 	if m.taskState == nil {
795: 		return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("no task state loaded"))
796: 	}
797: 	entry, ok := m.taskState.Plans[planFile]
798: 	if !ok {
799: 		return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("missing task state for %s", planFile))
800: 	}
801: 
802: 	// Backfill branch name for plans created before the branch field existed.
803: 	if entry.Branch == "" {
804: 		entry.Branch = gitpkg.TaskBranchFromFile(planFile)
805: 		if err := m.taskState.SetBranch(planFile, entry.Branch); err != nil {
806: 			return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("failed to assign branch for plan: %w", err))
807: 		}
808: 	}
809: 
810: 	switch stage {
811: 	case "plan":
812: 		if err := m.fsm.Transition(planFile, taskfsm.PlanStart); err != nil {
813: 			return m, m.handleError(err)
814: 		}
815: 		m.audit(auditlog.EventPlanTransition, string(entry.Status)+" → planning",
816: 			auditlog.WithPlan(planFile))
817: 		m.loadTaskState()
818: 		m.updateSidebarTasks()
819: 		return m.spawnTaskAgent(planFile, "plan", buildPlanningPrompt(taskstate.DisplayName(planFile), entry.Description))
820: 	case "solo":
821: 		// Check store content before fsmSetImplementing — the FSM transition calls
822: 		// store.Update which overwrites the content field with an empty string.
823: 		// Reading before the transition preserves any ingested plan content.
824: 		planName := taskstate.DisplayName(planFile)
825: 		refFile := ""
826: 		if m.taskStore != nil {
827: 			if c, err := m.taskStore.GetContent(m.taskStoreProject, planFile); err == nil && c != "" {
828: 				refFile = planFile
829: 			}
830: 		}
831: 		if err := m.fsmSetImplementing(planFile); err != nil {
832: 			return m, m.handleError(err)
833: 		}
834: 		m.audit(auditlog.EventPlanTransition, string(entry.Status)+" → implementing (solo)",
835: 			auditlog.WithPlan(planFile))
836: 		m.loadTaskState()
837: 		m.updateSidebarTasks()
838: 		prompt := buildSoloPrompt(planName, entry.Description, refFile)
839: 		return m.spawnTaskAgent(planFile, "solo", prompt)
840: 	case "implement":
841: 		// Read and parse plan from the task store — this also validates wave headers.
842: 		rawContent := ""
843: 		if m.taskStore != nil {
844: 			c, err := m.taskStore.GetContent(m.taskStoreProject, planFile)
845: 			if err != nil {
846: 				return m, m.handleError(err)
847: 			}
848: 			rawContent = c
849: 		}

906: // handleTmuxBrowserAction dispatches actions from the tmux session browser overlay.
907: // browser is the TmuxBrowserOverlay captured BEFORE HandleKey was called (so SelectedItem is valid).
908: // action is the Result.Action string returned by HandleKey.
909: func (m *home) handleTmuxBrowserAction(browser *overlay.TmuxBrowserOverlay, action string) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
910: 	switch action {
911: 	case "": // dismissed without action
912: 		m.overlays.Dismiss()
913: 		m.state = stateDefault
914: 		return m, nil
915: 
916: 	case "kill":
917: 		if browser == nil {
918: 			return m, nil
919: 		}
920: 		item := browser.SelectedItem()
921: 		if item.Name == "" {
922: 			return m, nil
923: 		}
924: 		name := item.Name
925: 		browser.RemoveSelected()
926: 		if browser.IsEmpty() {
927: 			m.overlays.Dismiss()
928: 			m.state = stateDefault
929: 		}
930: 		return m, func() tea.Msg {
931: 			killCmd := exec.Command("tmux", "kill-session", "-t", name)
932: 			err := killCmd.Run()
933: 			return tmuxKillResultMsg{name: name, err: err}
934: 		}
935: 
936: 	case "adopt":
937: 		if browser == nil {
938: 			return m, nil
939: 		}
940: 		item := browser.SelectedItem()
941: 		if item.Name == "" {
942: 			return m, nil
943: 		}
944: 		m.state = stateDefault
945: 		return m.adoptOrphanSession(overlay.TmuxBrowserItem{
946: 			Name:  item.Name,
947: 			Title: item.Title,
948: 		})
949: 
950: 	case "attach":
951: 		if browser == nil {
952: 			return m, nil
953: 		}
954: 		item := browser.SelectedItem()
955: 		if item.Name == "" {
956: 			return m, nil
957: 		}
958: 		m.state = stateDefault
959: 		name := item.Name
960: 		return m, tea.ExecProcess(exec.Command("tmux", "attach-session", "-t", name), func(err error) tea.Msg {
961: 			return tmuxAttachReturnMsg{}
962: 		})
963: 
964: 	default:
965: 		return m, nil
966: 	}
967: }
968: 
969: // isLocked returns true if the given stage cannot be triggered given the current plan status.
970: // The context menu already gates which forward stages are offered, so this only
971: // guards against truly nonsensical transitions (e.g. marking "finished" when already done).
972: func isLocked(status taskstate.Status, stage string) bool {
973: 	switch stage {
974: 	case "plan", "implement", "solo", "review":
975: 		// Forward progression is always allowed — the FSM helpers
976: 		// (fsmSetImplementing, fsmSetReviewing) walk through intermediate states.
977: 		return false
978: 	case "finished":
979: 		return status != taskstate.StatusReviewing
980: 	default:
981: 		return true
982: 	}
983: }

20: func sanitizeBranchName(s string) string {
21: 	s = strings.ToLower(s)
22: 	s = strings.ReplaceAll(s, " ", "-")
23: 	s = unsafeCharsRegex.ReplaceAllString(s, "")

32: 	if _, err := exec.LookPath("gh"); err != nil {
33: 		return fmt.Errorf("GitHub CLI (gh) is not installed. Please install it first")
34: 	}
35: 
36: 	// Check if gh is authenticated
37: 	cmd := exec.Command("gh", "auth", "status")
38: 	if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
39: 		return fmt.Errorf("GitHub CLI is not configured. Please run 'gh auth login' first")
40: 	}

45: // IsGitRepo checks if the given path is within a git repository
46: func IsGitRepo(path string) bool {

48: 	return err == nil
49: }
50: 
51: func findGitRepoRoot(path string) (string, error) {
52: 	currentPath := path
53: 	for {
54: 		_, err := git.PlainOpen(currentPath)
55: 		if err == nil {
56: 			// Found the repository root
57: 			return currentPath, nil
58: 		}
59: 
60: 		parent := filepath.Dir(currentPath)
61: 		if parent == currentPath {
62: 			// Reached the filesystem root without finding a repository
63: 			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to find Git repository root from path: %s", path)
64: 		}
65: 		currentPath = parent
66: 	}
67: }

271: // findInstanceData finds an instance record by title. It first tries an exact
272: // match, then falls back to a substring match. Returns an error when no match
273: // is found or when the substring matches more than one record (ambiguous).
274: func findInstanceData(records []instanceRecord, title string) (instanceRecord, error) {
275: 	// Exact match takes precedence.

277: 		if r.Title == title {

279: 		}

284: 	for _, r := range records {

310: func validateStatusForAction(data instanceRecord, action string) error {

479: 			// Commit any dirty changes in the worktree before pausing.

498: 				return nil

parts.text:
Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me summarize what exists and what's needed:

**What exists:**
- `kas task list` — lists tasks with optional status filter
- `kas task register` — registers a plan file from disk
- `kas task set-status` — force-overrides status (requires --force)
- `kas task transition` — applies FSM events
- `kas task implement` — triggers wave implementation
- `kas task show` — prints plan content
- `kas task link-clickup` — backfills ClickUp IDs

**What's missing (the goal):**


I need to build several new commands: `create` for making tasks directly, `start` to initialize implementation with branch and worktree setup, `push` and `pr` for remote operations, `merge` to finalize tasks, and `start-over` to reset progress. I can leverage existing utilities like `EnsureTaskBranch()` and `MergeTaskBranch()` from the session/git layer to avoid duplicating logic.

The TUI already handles all these operations through context menus, so the CLI just needs to wire up the same underlying functions. For `create`, I'll generate a minimal task entry directly in the store rather than reading from disk like `register` does.

Looking at the existing `implement` command, it transitions to "implementing" and writes a signal file, but the worktree creation happens separately when spawning agents. So the CLI lifecycle should be: `create` to add a new task with optional metadata, then `start` to transition to implementing and set up the branch and worktree.

The remaining commands follow naturally — `push` commits and pushes the branch, `merge` integrates it back to main, `pr` opens a GitHub PR, and `start-over` resets everything back to planning. But I'm realizing the current `kas task` commands are lower-level than what the user is asking for. -level (transition, set-status, implement). The new commands are high-level lifecycle operations that wrap git + FSM + worktree operations together. This is the "porcelain" layer on top of the existing "plumbing".

Now let me plan this properly.

For `kas task create <name>`, I'll create a new task entry in the store with "ready" status, derive the branch name from the task name, and optionally accept description, branch, topic, or content from stdin or file—unlike `register`, this doesn't require a file already on disk.

`kas task start <plan-file>` validates the task is in a startable state, transitions it to implementing, creates the git branch and worktree checkout, then outputs the worktree path for the user.

`kas task push <plan-file>` finds the task's branch, resolves the worktree, commits any dirty changes with an auto-generated message, and pushes to origin without needing TUI confirmation.

`kas task merge <plan-file>` validates the task exists, removes the worktree, merges the branch, deletes it, transitions to done, and prints success.

`kas task pr <plan-file>` finds the task's branch, pushes it first, then creates a PR using... The worktree path comes from the task's branch using `TaskWorktreePath(repoRoot, branch)`, and whether commands can run depends on whether that worktree exists — `push` and `pr` require it, while `merge` and `start-over` work from the main repo. These commands are all independent cobra subcommands, so I can structure them as separate waves in the plan.

Looking at the scope, I need to implement six task commands: `create`, `start`, `push`, `merge`, `pr`, and `start-over`. Each one requires a new execute function, cobra command, and tests. Since they share common patterns like resolving the repo root, loading task state, and setting up git worktree objects, I should be able to handle them in a single wave despite the number of tasks involved. into `cmd/task.go` and `cmd/task_test.go`. They'll also need small helper functions.

This is a Medium-sized plan. Each command is fairly straightforward since the building blocks exist in `session/git/`. But there are 6 commands, so I should think about wave structure.

Actually, the commands share a common pattern. Let me group them:

**Wave 1**: Core helpers + `create` + `start` (foundation commands)
**Wave 2**: `push` + `merge` + `pr` + `start-over` (git lifecycle commands that depend on tasks existing)

Wait — do they really have a dependency? The tests for push/merge/pr need a task to exist, but they can create one in the test setup. These are unit tests with in-memory stores. There's no real dependency between the tasks themselves.

Actually, all 6 commands are independent. They all modify `cmd/task.go` and `cmd/task_test.go`, which means I can't put them in the same wave due to file conflicts. I need to split them across multiple tasks instead.

Let me group them into 2-3 tasks that each touch the same files but handle different commands: `create` and `start` together as the bootstrap commands, then `push` and `pr` as the remote git operations, and finally `merge` and `start-over` as the remaining lifecycle commands. I'm realizing that both tasks would need to modify `cmd/task.go`, which violates the wave constraint. So I'm stuck with either merging everything into one task or accepting the 2-wave structure—and since these are distinct functional areas (entry points vs. git operations), splitting them across waves makes sense even if it feels a bit rigid for what's essentially adding subcommands to an existing tree. , they must be in 2 waves if they both touch the same file. That's fine.

Let me design the 2 tasks:

### Task 1: `create` + `start` commands + shared helpers
Files: cmd/task.go, cmd/task_test.go
- Add `resolveTaskEntry` helper (shared lookup pattern)
- Add `executeTaskCreate` function
- Add `executeTaskStart` function
- Add cobra subcommands
- Add tests

### Task 2: `push` + `merge` + `pr` + `start-over` commands
Files: cmd/task.go, cmd/task_test.go
- Add `executeTaskPush` function
- Add `executeTaskMerge` function
- Add `executeTaskPR` function
- Add `executeTaskStartOver` function
- Add cobra subcommands
- Add tests

Actually, let me reconsider the scope. Four commands in one task might be pushing it—each command needs roughly 20 lines of implementation plus 20 lines of tests, so that's around 160 lines total and probably 35-40 minutes of work. That fits within the 45-minute window, but I should double-check what `start` actually requires. Looking at the implementation, it needs to load the task state, find the entry by plan file, determine the branch name, and handle the case where the branch isn't set yet.

Implementing the FSM transitions...

I need to finish setting up the worktree and return its path. The `Setup()` method handles the actual checkout, whether it's setting up a new worktree or using an existing branch. I can compute the worktree path before calling `Setup()` since `TaskWorktreePath` gives me that information upfront, so I should be able to return the path once the setup completes successfully.

For testing the `start` command, I don't need to actually invoke the git worktree creation — I can focus on testing the FSM transitions from ready to implementing, branch name resolution, and error handling like missing tasks or invalid states. The existing test patterns show this approach works well, where the FSM logic and file operations are tested without requiring a real git repository.

I'm thinking through the overall implementation plan now: first wave covers `create` and `start` commands with their execute functions and tests, then the second wave tackles `push`, `merge`, `pr`, and `start-over` commands.

For the `create` command specifically, I'm considering how it differs from `register` — `register` loads task definitions from files in `docs/plans/`, while `create` should build a task entry directly with optional content passed via stdin, a file path, or an inline flag.

Now I'm thinking through how `start` should work as a headless alternative to `implement` — it needs to transition the task to implementing, set up the branch and worktree, then output the worktree path so users can work without the TUI.

For the `push` command, I need to construct a `GitWorktree` object from the CLI by looking up the task's branch, deriving the worktree path, verifying it exists on disk, then optionally committing changes before pushing. The `merge` command is simpler since it just calls a standalone function to merge the branch and update the FSM. For `pr`, I'd follow a similar pattern of looking up the branch and deriving the worktree path.

The key difference between TUI and CLI is that the TUI manages instances (killing them before operations), while the CLI skips that step and just performs the git operations and FSM transitions directly. Now I'm thinking through the testing strategy, starting with how to verify the `create` command works correctly.

For `start`, I need to test FSM transitions and branch resolution without hitting actual git operations—I can use a temporary repository for this. The `push` and `merge` commands are trickier since they depend on real git state, so I'll focus on testing the happy path and error handling logic. The `pr` command requires the GitHub CLI which is hard to unit test, so I'll verify the command construction instead. For `start-over`, I just need to confirm the FSM transitions back to planning.

I'm planning to test the `execute*` functions directly since they take the store as an argument and are more testable than the cobra command wrappers. Let me check the `worktree.go` file to understand what else I need to account for.

12: // getWorktreeDirectory returns the directory used to store git worktrees for

14: func getWorktreeDirectory(repoPath string) (string, error) {

16: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("repo path is required for worktree directory")
17: 	}

19: }

27: 	baseCommitSHA string
28: }

31: // state without any filesystem interaction. Used when restoring from storage.
32: func NewGitWorktreeFromStorage(repoPath, worktreePath, sessionName, branchName, baseCommitSHA string) *GitWorktree {
33: 	return &GitWorktree{
34: 		repoPath:      repoPath,
35: 		worktreePath:  worktreePath,

43: // derived from the configured branch prefix and the session name.

<content>1: package git
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 	"os"
6: 	"strings"
7: )
8: 
9: // DiffStats holds the result of a diff computation between the worktree and its base commit.
10: type DiffStats struct {
11: 	// Content is the full raw diff output.
12: 	Content string
13: 	// Added is the count of lines added (lines starting with "+" but not "+++").
14: 	Added int
15: 	// Removed is the count of lines removed (lines starting with "-" but not "---").
16: 	Removed int
17: 	// Error captures any error encountered during diff computation so callers
18: 	// can inspect it without the method panicking or returning a nil pointer.
19: 	Error error
20: }
21: 
22: // IsEmpty reports whether the diff has no changes and no content.
23: func (d *DiffStats) IsEmpty() bool {
24: 	return d.Added == 0 && d.Removed == 0 && d.Content == ""
25: }
26: 
27: // Diff computes the diff between the worktree's current state and its base commit.
28: // It returns a populated DiffStats in all cases; callers should check DiffStats.Error.
29: func (g *GitWorktree) Diff() *DiffStats {
30: 	stats := &DiffStats{}
31: 
32: 	if _, err := os.Stat(g.worktreePath); err != nil {
33: 		stats.Error = fmt.Errorf("worktree path unavailable: %w", err)
34: 		return stats
35: 	}
36: 
37: 	base := g.GetBaseCommitSHA()
38: 	if base == "" {
39: 		stats.Error = fmt.Errorf("base commit SHA not available")
40: 		return stats
41: 	}
42: 
43: 	output, err := g.runGitCommand(g.worktreePath, "--no-pager", "diff", base)
44: 	if err != nil {
45: 		stats.Error = err
46: 		return stats
47: 	}
48: 
49: 	for _, line := range strings.Split(output, "\n") {
50: 		switch {
51: 		case strings.HasPrefix(line, "+") && !strings.HasPrefix(line, "+++"):
52: 			stats.Added++
53: 		case strings.HasPrefix(line, "-") && !strings.HasPrefix(line, "---"):
54: 			stats.Removed++
55: 		}
56: 	}
57: 	stats.Content = output
58: 
59: 	return stats
60: }

(End of file - total 60 lines)
</content>
